title: limonium graecum subsp. divaricatum
intro: Limonium graecum subsp. divaricatum, also known as sea lavender or divaricate sea lavender, is a flowering plant species within the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Limonium graecum subsp. divaricatum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it is often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
